brief_justification: Directly identifies self-selection as the critical flaw and explains why the 18-point effect likely doesn't transfer to mandated use — exactly matching the ground truth; traces second and third-order effects (unmeasured confounders → mandate removes selection mechanism → displaced instructional time), going well beyond the surface claim; proceeds linearly with no explicit reflection on its own reasoning method or bias checks; mentions RCT as the corrective design but does not apply verification to its own claims or test boundary conditions of the self-selection argument; uses 'likely,' 'probably,' and 'possibly' throughout but does not structure the uncertainty or identify what evidence would change the conclusion; identifies only the self-selection assumption without evaluating competing critical assumptions (e.g., effect homogeneity across demographics, platform content quality, displacement effects on other learning); every conclusion traces clearly to prior premises, and a third party could reproduce the full argument chain from the response alone.
